Tonight's guest is Dylan.
Dylan, welcome to the show.
Thank you, Vic.
Well, thanks so much for coming on.

Dylan, please give us a brief bio on yourself.
Well, I live in Florida.
I am 20 years old and I'm 6'3", 6'4", and I weigh about 300 pounds.
And for one of these stories, my vehicle I drive is a 1991 Bronco.
And that's about all.
And you've got a cattle farm, which for most eyewitnesses, that would definitely complicate things.
But we're going to get into that in just a bit here.
Before we do get into that, though, you were just nine years old when you had your first encounter.
Did that have an effect on your interest in doing things in the woods and the outdoors?
For a little while, yes, it did.
But I got over it.
I started going out into the woods with a small group of people.
I wouldn't necessarily go out by myself, but I would slowly start going back into the woods
with a small group of people, and eventually I got to where I was able to go by myself
out into the woods.
Oh, good.
I'm glad you're able to get back to that point.
Yeah, that's really good.
Knowing what's out there in the dark, and sometimes in the daytime as well, do
you always insist upon being armed whenever you go into the woods or even close to it?
I would say yes, but there's not a lot of things that you can carry without it
being noticed when you go into the woods.
Like small arms, that's easy to carry, but anything that could possibly put a hurting
on anything that happened in these encounters, I would not have been able to carry quietly
or keep it at least hidden.

If you want to carry anything that's going to do any good against a dogman, it's going
to be way too unwieldy to do that with any kind of comfort.

on a ranch where you live.

you're tending to the animals.

compared to not having that on.

I really do.

Beef.
